source_pr.title: ddl: speed up add index with split tables
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/26683 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:
The `SHARD_ROW_ID_BITS` value or `AUTO_RANDOM` is set and a large amount of data is inserted into the table. Later, adding an index to the table, in a few cases, cause the backfill index to be slower than the write speed of the data in the table.

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

How it Works:
In the original method, each worker originally adds indexes to the region range [startHandle, endHandle] by batch. In the new method, we get the range of actual data in each region to add indexes on a batch basis.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Start cluster with `tiup playground --tiflash 0` in local.

### case 1
```
create table tbl(a bigint, b bigint, b bigint) SHARD_ROW_ID_BITS = 4 pre_split_regions = 4;
```
Add 2 million rows to this table.
Then connect1 inserts 20 million rows(50 goroutines * 400 k), connect2 executes alter table tbl add index idx(a);.

Before this PR(**connect1 finished insert operation, 14 min 33 sec**):
```
tidb> alter table tbl add index idx(c1);
Query OK, 0 rows affected (22 min 58.35 sec)
```

After this PR(**add index backfill 6 million rows**):
```
tidb> alter table tbl add index idx(c1);
Query OK, 0 rows affected (12 min 52.16 sec)
```
### case 2
```
CREATE TABLE tbl(a bigint PRIMARY KEY AUTO_RANDOM(4), b varchar(255), c bigint);
SPLIT TABLE tbl BETWEEN (-9223372036854775808) AND (9223372036854775807) REGIONS 16;
```
Add 2 million rows to this table.
Then connect1 inserts 5 million rows(50 goroutines * 100 k), connect2 executes alter table tbl add index idx(a);.

Before this PR(**connect1 finished insert operation, 4 min 20 sec**):
```
tidb> alter table tbl add index idx(c1);
Query OK, 0 rows affected (4 min 54.72 sec)
```

After this PR(**connect1 finished insert operation, 3 min 21 sec**):
```
tidb> alter table tbl add index idx(c1);
Query OK, 0 rows affected (6 min 40.69 sec)
```
In theory, this PR doesn't speed up the case particularly well, not as well as the previous case.

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

```release-note
Optimize ”Add Index” for an explicit split region table 
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky around DDL/schema changes where schema propagation is asynchronous. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("select * from test_get order by a").Check(testkit.Rows("-9223372036854775808 -9223372036854775808", (ddl/serial_test.go).
